title: 10 best herbal teas for sunburn
intro_desc: Herbal teas for sunburn are infusions made from plants that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort and redness caused by sunburn. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for sunburn include chamomile, calendula, and lavender. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling, while calendula tea has been shown to promote skin healing and lavender tea has a calming effect that can help soothe irritated skin. By using these herbal teas as a compress or adding them to a bath, individuals can find relief from the pain and discomfort of sunburn, allowing them to enjoy their summer activities without worrying about the negative effects of sun exposure.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tea helps with sunburn because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory compounds called polyphenols, which can help reduce inflammation and redness in sunburned skin. Additionally, aloe vera has antioxidant properties that can help repair damage caused by UV radiation and promote skin healing. The tea is also hydrating, which can help soothe and moisturize sunburned skin, preventing further dryness and peeling. Drinking aloe vera tea can also help from the inside out, as it can help boost the immune system and promote overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera tea contains compounds like bradykinase, which helps reduce inflammation, providing relief from sunburn pain and redness.
Moisturizing: Aloe vera tea is rich in polysaccharides and water, making it an effective moisturizer that helps soothe and hydrate sunburnt skin.
Antioxidant: Aloe vera tea has antioxidant properties due to its content of vitamins A, C, and E, which help protect skin from free radical damage caused by sun exposure.
Wound healing: Aloe vera tea contains compounds like gibberellins and auxins, which promote wound healing and tissue regeneration, aiding in sunburn recovery.
Pain relief: Aloe vera tea's analgesic properties, derived from compounds like salicylic acid and magnesium lactate, help alleviate sunburn-related pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the aloe vera plant contain a gel-like substance that is rich in compounds with anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, making it effective for soothing sunburned skin.
Stems: The stems of the aloe vera plant contain a sap that can be used to make medicinal tea, and this sap has been shown to have skin-healing and sunburn-relieving properties.

Flowers: The flowers of the aloe vera plant contain nectar that can be used to make a medicinal tea, and this tea has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, which can help to alleviate the discomfort of sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Slice two leaves from an aloe vera plant, then use a knife to scrape the gel into a pot, adding 2 cups of water and 1 tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes to allow the chamomile to infuse.
Strain the tea into a teapot or container, discarding the used aloe vera gel and chamomile flowers.
Add honey or lemon juice to taste, if desired, and let the tea cool down to a warm temperature that's comfortable for sunburned skin.
Soak a clean cloth in the tea and apply it to the sunburned areas for 10-15 minutes, repeating as needed every few hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with sunburn because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and heal damaged skin. The tea is rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV rays. Additionally, pot marigold tea has been shown to promote the production of new skin cells, which can aid in the healing process of sunburned skin. The tea can be applied topically to the affected area or consumed as a warm beverage to help reduce inflammation and promote overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea helps reduce inflammation caused by sunburn, soothing the skin and providing relief from redness and swelling. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in pot marigold tea helps protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, which are produced by UV radiation from the sun. This property is attributed to the flavonoids and carotenoids found in the plant.

Wound healing: Pot marigold tea promotes wound healing, which is beneficial for sunburn as it helps the skin recover from the damage caused by UV radiation. This is due to the presence of the compound α-chamigrenic acid, which has been shown to have wound-healing properties.

Anti-bacterial: The antibacterial properties of pot marigold tea help prevent sunburn-related infections, ensuring the affected area remains clean and free from harmful bacteria. This property is attributed to the presence of essential oils in the plant, such as limonene and α-pinene.

Moisturizing: Pot marigold tea has moisturizing properties that help keep the skin hydrated and prevent dryness, which is essential for treating sunburn. This property is due to the mucilage content in the plant, which forms a protective layer on the skin and helps retain moisture.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for soothing sunburned skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant contain high levels of flavonoids, which can help reduce redness and irritation caused by sunburn.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids, which can help moisturize and soothe sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 dried marigold flowers and place them in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the marigold flowers, allowing them to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the flowers from the tea.
Optional: Add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day to soothe sunburned skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with sunburn because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and heal sunburned skin. The antioxidants in lavender tea can help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain, while also promoting skin regeneration. Additionally, lavender tea has been shown to have a calming effect on the mind and body, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety associated with sunburn. Drinking lavender tea or using it as a compress on the affected area can provide relief and accelerate the healing process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tea helps reduce inflammation and redness caused by sunburn due to the presence of compounds like linalyl acetate and linalool.
Pain-relieving: Its analgesic properties help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with sunburn, thanks to the active constituent, eucalyptol.
Wound-healing: Lavender tea promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which is beneficial for sunburned skin, because of its high antioxidant content and rosmarinic acid.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of English lavender tea help prevent infection in sunburned skin, as it contains compounds like camphor and tannins.
Soothing: Lavender tea has a calming effect on the skin, helping to soothe irritation and itchiness caused by sunburn, due to the presence of linalool and linalyl acetate.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, lavender flowers help reduce inflammation and promote healing for sunburned skin.
Leaves: Containing soothing properties, lavender leaves help alleviate pain and irritation caused by sunburn.
Buds: High in anti-inflammatory compounds, lavender buds aid in reducing redness and swelling from sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let 1 teaspoon of dried English lavender flowers steep for 10 minutes to extract the herb's soothing properties.
Strain the tea to remove the lavender flowers, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool down to a warm temperature that is comfortable to the touch.
Soak a clean cloth in the lavender tea and apply it to the sunburned area for 15 minutes.
Repeat the application every few hours as needed to relieve sunburn symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint tea helps with sunburn because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and heal damaged skin. The antioxidants found in spearmint tea, such as rosmarinic acid, help to reduce inflammation and redness, while also promoting the repair of sun-damaged skin cells. Additionally, spearmint tea has been shown to have a cooling effect on the skin, which can provide instant relief to sunburned areas. Drinking spearmint tea can also help to hydrate the body from the inside out, which is essential for healing sunburned skin. Overall, spearmint tea can be a natural and effective remedy for treating sunburn and promoting skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Spearmint tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling caused by sunburn.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of spearmint tea, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect skin cells from damage caused by UV radiation.
Analgesic: Spearmint tea has analgesic (pain-relieving) properties, primarily due to its menthol content, which can alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with sunburn.
Antimicrobial: Spearmint tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent bacterial infections that may occur as a result of damaged skin caused by sunburn.
Hydrating: Spearmint tea can help rehydrate and soothe sunburned skin, as it contains tannins and other compounds that can help restore the skin's natural moisture barrier.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Spearmint leaves are rich in antioxidants and menthol, which can help soothe sunburned skin and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: Spearmint flowers contain flavonoids and volatile oils that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them useful for treating sunburn pain and redness.
Seeds: Spearmint seeds are a good source of essential oils and fatty acids, which can help moisturize and repair sun-damaged skin.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 2-3 minutes.
Crush 1 tablespoon of dried spearmint leaves or use 2 tablespoons of fresh spearmint leaves.
Steep the crushed spearmint leaves in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before applying it to the sunburned skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with sunburn because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can soothe and heal damaged skin. The tea's antioxidants, such as rosmarinic acid, help to reduce inflammation and redness, while its anti-inflammatory properties can alleviate pain and promote skin regeneration. Moreover, lemon balm tea has been shown to have a cooling effect on the skin, which can provide immediate relief to sunburned areas. Its soothing properties can also help to reduce itching and prevent the formation of blisters. Drinking lemon balm tea can also help to boost the immune system and promote overall skin health, making it an effective remedy for sunburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tea contains rosmarinic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and redness caused by sunburn.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, helps protect skin cells from damage caused by UV radiation.
Pain-relieving: Lemon balm tea has analgesic properties, which can alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with sunburn.
Wound-healing: The presence of tannins in lemon balm tea can help promote wound healing and tissue regeneration, aiding in the recovery of sunburned skin.
Soothing: Lemon balm tea contains eugenol, a compound known for its calming and soothing effects, providing relief to sunburned skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are commonly used to make medicinal tea for sunburn due to their high content of rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant that helps soothe and heal sun-damaged skin.

Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant contain flavonoids and essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them useful for treating sunburn and reducing redness and pain.
Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, the stems of lemon balm tea plant still contain beneficial compounds that can help soothe and heal sunburned skin when used in medicinal tea.
remedy_recipe: Pick 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves or use 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the leaves from the water and let the tea cool down.
Optionally, add honey or lemon juice to taste.
Drink the tea or use it as a compress on the sunburned skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with sunburn because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and heal damaged skin. The rosmarinic acid and salvianolic acid found in sage tea have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can help alleviate the pain and redness associated with sunburn. Additionally, sage tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection in damaged skin. Drinking sage tea can also help hydrate the body and support overall skin health, which can aid in the healing process of sunburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage tea helps reduce inflammation caused by sunburn, soothing the skin and alleviating pain, thanks to the presence of rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, including rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, helps protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals generated by UV radiation.
Antimicrobial: Sage tea's antimicrobial properties, due to the presence of camphor and 1,8-cineole, help prevent bacterial infections that can occur as a result of sunburn-induced skin damage.
Astringent: Sage tea's astringent properties, from tannins, help to dry and tighten sunburned skin, reducing redness and swelling.
Analgesic: Sage tea's analgesic properties, attributed to various compounds such as ursolic acid and carnosic acid, help alleviate sunburn-related pain and discomfort by numbing the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, sage leaves help soothe sunburned skin and promote healing.
Flowers: Containing rosmarinic acid and other phenolic compounds, sage flowers aid in reducing skin irritation and redness caused by sunburn.
Stems: High in essential oils, sage stems provide a soothing aroma and help alleviate discomfort from sunburned skin.
Bark: With tannins that have astringent properties, sage bark can help protect and repair sun-damaged skin.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried sage leaves to the boiling water, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the sage leaves, then let it cool down to a warm temperature.
Add a little bit of honey or lemon juice for taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day for sunburn relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with sunburn because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can soothe and heal damaged skin. The herb's active ingredients, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn. Additionally, thyme tea has antimicrobial properties that can prevent infection in broken skin caused by severe sunburn. Drinking thyme tea can also help hydrate the body and promote overall skin health, which can aid in the recovery process after sun damage. Overall, thyme tea's healing properties make it a natural and effective remedy for sunburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea helps reduce inflammation caused by sunburn, soothing the skin and alleviating pain. This property is due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antioxidant: Thyme tea contains antioxidants that help protect skin cells from damage caused by UV radiation, promoting healing and skin regeneration. The primary antioxidants in thyme tea are rosmarinic acid and luteolin-7-O-glucoside.

Antimicrobial: Thyme tea's antimicrobial properties help prevent infection in sunburned skin, protecting wounds from bacteria and other pathogens. The main antimicrobial compounds in thyme tea are thymol and carvacrol.

Wound healing: Thyme tea promotes wound healing by increasing blood flow and tissue regeneration, which helps sunburned skin recover more quickly. The active constituents responsible for this property include ursolic acid and oleanolic acid.

Analgesic: Thyme tea has pain-relieving properties that can alleviate the discomfort associated with sunburn. This is due to the presence of compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have been shown to have analgesic effects.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for soothing sunburned skin.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain flavonoids that help reduce redness and irritation caused by sunburn.
Stems: Thyme stems have essential oils that possess skin-healing and soothing qualities, beneficial for sunburn relief.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1-2 teaspoons of dried thyme leaves and place them in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the thyme leaves.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the herb's beneficial compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the leaves.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before gently applying it to sunburned skin using a clean cloth or spray bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet basil tea helps with sunburn because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can soothe and heal damaged skin. The tea's cooling effect can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with sunburn, while its high concentration of essential oils, such as eugenol and linalool, can reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation. Moreover, sweet basil tea is rich in vitamin A and C, which can promote the production of collagen and help repair sun-damaged skin. Drinking sweet basil tea can also help hydrate the body and replenish fluids lost due to sun exposure, further aiding in the healing process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sweet basil tea can reduce inflammation and swelling, which is important for sunburn as it helps alleviate the pain and redness caused by damaged skin. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as eugenol, citronellol, and linalool.

Antioxidant: The tea has antioxidants that help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, which are increased in number due to sun exposure. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and orientin.

Wound healing: Sweet basil tea promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which is crucial for sunburn as it helps repair the damaged skin. This is attributed to the presence of ursolic acid and rosmarinic acid.

Analgesic: The tea has pain-relieving properties, which is important for sunburn as it helps alleviate the discomfort caused by sunburn. The active constituents responsible for this property include eugenol and linalool.

Antimicrobial: Sweet basil tea has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection of the damaged skin, which is important for sunburn as it reduces the risk of complications. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as eugenol, citronellol, and linalool.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe sunburned skin.
Flowers: Rich in essential oils and antioxidants, they can help reduce redness and inflammation caused by sunburn.
Seeds: Packed with nutrients, they can help promote skin healing and provide relief from sunburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Pick 2 tablespoons of fresh sweet basil leaves, rinse and let them air dry.
Boil 2 cups of water, then add the dried basil leaves and let it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the basil leaves, let it cool down to a warm temperature.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of honey for added anti-inflammatory benefits and sweetness.
Drink the tea or use it as a compress, applying it to the sunburned area for relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with sunburn because it contains antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and heal damaged skin. The rosmarinic acid found in rosemary works to reduce inflammation and redness, while its antioxidants help to repair and protect skin cells from further damage caused by UV rays. Additionally, rosemary tea has been shown to improve circulation, which can help to speed up the healing process and reduce the appearance of sunburn. Drinking rosemary tea or applying it topically as a compress can provide relief and promote skin recovery after sun exposure.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary tea helps reduce inflammation caused by sunburn, soothing the skin and alleviating pain. This property is due to the presence of compounds like rosmarinic acid and carnosol.

Antioxidant: Rosemary tea contains potent antioxidants that help protect skin cells from damage caused by UV radiation, promoting skin health and accelerating healing. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid, carnosol, and caffeic acid.

Analgesic: Rosemary tea has pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort associated with sunburn. The compound responsible for this effect is primarily attributed to the essential oil, 1,8-cineole.

Improves Circulation: Rosemary tea promotes blood flow, which helps expedite the healing process and reduces the appearance of sunburn. The active constituents responsible for this property are rosmarinic acid and the essential oil α-pinene.

Antimicrobial: Rosemary tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection in damaged skin caused by sunburn. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are the essential oils α-pinene, camphene, and limonene.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the rosemary tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for soothing sunburned skin.
Flowers: The flowers of the rosemary tea plant contain high levels of antioxidants and have been shown to help reduce redness and irritation, making them useful for treating sunburn.
Stems: The stems of the rosemary tea plant contain essential oils that have been shown to have a cooling effect on the skin, making them useful for relieving the discomfort of sunburn.
Seeds: The seeds of the rosemary tea plant are rich in essential fatty acids and antioxidants, making them useful for reducing inflammation and promoting skin healing.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5-10 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried rosemary leaves to the boiling water, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the rosemary leaves, then let it cool down to a warm temperature.
Optionally, add honey to taste for a natural sweetener.
Drink the rosemary tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and heal sunburned skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley tea helps with sunburn because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn. The herb is rich in antioxidants like vitamin C, which can help repair damaged skin cells and promote the growth of new ones. Additionally, parsley has cooling properties that can soothe irritated skin and provide instant relief from the discomfort of sunburn. Drinking parsley tea can also help hydrate the body, which is essential for healthy skin and can aid in the healing process of sunburn. Overall, parsley tea is a natural and effective remedy for sunburn that can provide quick relief and support the skin's natural healing process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Parsley tea helps reduce inflammation caused by sunburn, soothing the skin and alleviating pain, due to its high content of flavonoids and volatile oils such as myristicin and apiol.
Antioxidant: The tea's antioxidant properties, derived from rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids, help protect skin cells from damage caused by UV radiation and support the healing process.
Diuretic: Parsley tea promotes diuresis, helping the body eliminate toxins and reducing swelling caused by sunburn, thanks to the presence of apiol and myristicin.
Vitamin-rich: Rich in vitamins A, C, and K, parsley tea aids in skin repair, boosts collagen production, and reduces the appearance of sunburn damage, promoting overall skin health.

Antimicrobial: Parsley tea's antimicrobial properties, attributed to its essential oils and tannins, help prevent bacterial infections in sunburned skin, reducing the risk of complications and promoting faster healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, parsley leaves help soothe sunburned skin and promote healing.
Seeds: Containing high levels of essential oils, parsley seeds can be used to make a medicinal tea that helps reduce redness and irritation caused by sunburn.
Flowers: With natural cooling properties, parsley flowers can be steeped in hot water to create a soothing tea for sunburn relief.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried parsley leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the parsley leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a warm temperature before applying it to sunburned skin.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat sunburn with herbal teas is by using cooled tea bags directly on the affected area. Chamomile and calendula teas are ideal for soothing sunburns due to their anti-inflammatory properties. Steep several tea bags in boiling water, let it cool, and then gently apply the tea bags to the sunburn. For a more potent remedy, create a strong tea by using a larger quantity of tea leaves and follow the same application process. Repeat this treatment several times a day for best results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to sunburn that are treated with herbal teas include sun poisoning, windburn, and rosacea. Chamomile tea is often used as a compress to soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation. Calendula tea is also known for its healing properties and can be used to treat minor burns, cuts, and skin irritations. For windburn, lavender tea can be applied topically to moisturize and repair damaged skin. Green tea, rich in antioxidants, can help reduce redness and accelerate skin healing. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal teas for medicinal purposes, especially for severe or persistent skin conditions.
